title: miyamura interviews translation
wikitext:
[]: miyamura interviews translation             Quote Post by 惣流・アスカ・ラングレー » Tue Apr 13, 2021 1:37 am INTERVIEW WITH ANIMATE TIMES a portion from this interview which isn’t present in the other interview. --In a way, you yourself have been saved by this "Rebuild" series. Miyamura: It really is. I don't know what Anno-san's intentions were in directing this film, but I was glad that he let Shinji talk to me in certain scenes, and I felt a kind of relief. However, when Ogata-san told us at the stage greeting that the other Shinji was staying behind after sending everyone off, we felt a strong desire to pick him up as an adult Asuka! I also had a strong feeling of "I want to pick him up as an adult Asuka! I'm sure that Shinji will be accompanied by many people, including the adult Asuka and Yui, but I'd like to leave the rest of the story to the fans (laughs).

[]: Re: miyamura interviews translation             Quote Post by Raikyu » Tue Apr 13, 2021 8:06 am Thanks a lot for this translation!!

[]: Re: miyamura interviews translation             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Apr 13, 2021 2:10 pm That sounds to me like it's deliberately ambiguous and the 'father' thing is Miyamura's own take. At least, we can#t pretend that the makers didn't know how it would be read but neither did they explicitly confirm anything. In this case maybe looking at the character as like being your kid isn't helpful, kids grow up eventually. At worst Shikinami is like a person with a delayed-puberty hormone disorder like turner syndrome. ppl with those get into relationships all the time; There are some that might render you effectively indistinguishable from an asexual or romantic person you'd still be an adult in the "can sign contracts and make sensible decisions" fashion. All the other interviews reference the fact that 'everyone but Shinji grew up'. As far as platonic readings go what makes the most sense to me is really the "A friend that she couldn't get rid off" line (I'm not even sure which of the of the two said it) - like she just felt depressed being around normal people, being reminded of what she lost or possibly never had, she feels she's mistrusted and while she is, in fact, wearing an explosive collar, it seems some of that isolation is self-imposed, as self-isolating is a common traumatized veterans to do. Her transformation gives her a seemingly sensible reason for why she can't go with the village & seems to confirm exactly what that trauma response would be telling her. I do like the nuance here that this doesn't keep her from being mature enough to feel she wants to protect the village despite her horrible fate, so she's not completely ruled by resentment or devoid of purpose, it's actually very impressive for all that it's also a partial continuation of this complex that she must do everything alone brought forth by her lonesome competitive upbringing. So I'd read this like Asuka enlisted at a young age & got the full mental & physical trauma package, & Kensuke is the last friend from her youth who she hasn't lost touch with/ managed to push away, a kind of vestigial support system. It should also be noted that for all her talk of being a big loner, she actually has a roommate both in the village and on the Wunder; She's probably one of those people who find it stressful to have no roommates.

[]: Re: miyamura interviews translation             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Tue Apr 13, 2021 8:36 pm Hey, thank you Asuka! This was a fairly interesting read. Miyamura is on fire! She's delivering a great interview after another. Kendrix wrote: That sounds to me like it's deliberately ambiguous and the 'father' thing is Miyamura's own take.

[]: Re: miyamura interviews translation             Quote Post by bogusman » Wed Apr 14, 2021 1:42 am 惣流・アスカ・ラングレー wrote: INTERVIEW WITH ANIMATE TIMES a portion from this interview which isn’t present in the other interview. --In a way, you yourself have been saved by this "Rebuild" series. Miyamura: It really is. I don't know what Anno-san's intentions were in directing this film, but I was glad that he let Shinji talk to me in certain scenes, and I felt a kind of relief. However, when Ogata-san told us at the stage greeting that the other Shinji was staying behind after sending everyone off, we felt a strong desire to pick him up as an adult Asuka! I also had a strong feeling of "I want to pick him up as an adult Asuka! I'm sure that Shinji will be accompanied by many people, including the adult Asuka and Yui, but I'd like to leave the rest of the story to the fans (laughs).

[]: Re: miyamura interviews translation             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Sat Apr 24, 2021 6:35 pm AnimaTimes and Movie Walker interviews are indeed entirely different. There's also this one, though it seems to need a Japanese VPN. https://www.crank-in.net/interview/88385 AnimateTimes is here: https://wiki.evageeks.org/Evangelion_3. ... imateTimes

[]: Re: miyamura interviews translation             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Apr 24, 2021 7:46 pm FelipeFritschF wrote: AnimateTimes is here: https://wiki.evageeks.org/Evangelion_3. ... imateTimes

[]: Re: miyamura interviews translation             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Sat Apr 24, 2021 8:07 pm She says she was instructed to play Sohryu and Shikinami as separate in her audio stream, too, so it's not just her interpretation. I also linked an earlier version of that on Tiffany's fan group. She was very happy to hear it.

[]: Re: miyamura interviews translation             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Sat Apr 24, 2021 9:02 pm Kendrix wrote: *laughs in team blue *

[]: Re: miyamura interviews translation             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Apr 24, 2021 9:10 pm Rei is not Yui. This one even less then usual since Gendo "didn't bother to transfer the data", not to mention how the movie continued to hammer down on their very different agendas here. I never said anything about "lover". Why is Friend not enough? Not to mention that she*s like the one person who's still around Shinji's age (if anything, ReiQ comes off younger) so she's the furthest from 'mother' out of anyone. This isn't even about anything ship related; If you don't get that she's her own person (no matter what Gendo might want) you've missed the whole point.

[]: Re: miyamura interviews translation             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Sat Apr 24, 2021 9:23 pm Kendrix wrote: I never said anything about "lover". Why is Friend not enough?

[]: Re: miyamura interviews translation             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Apr 24, 2021 9:39 pm I don't see what about her actions specifically suggests "mother" and not just general niceness. If you said "little sister" then I'd maybe remotely see where you're coming from, (though I might perhaps say that it takes a long time to build that sort of bond & few ppl call their siblings by their last names & that it still seems a tad shoehorned if not nearly as much as the 'mother' thing) You might as well argue that Asuka is a "mother" cause she feeds him some food. They're basically two random stray orphans whom the village people take in at the same time, she adapts slightly faster but she's still fundamentally another kid that's being taken care of & their getting to know that unfamiliar place at the same time (in contrasting ways) Depending on how "connected" you want to read the various Rei incarnations you could say that she's 'remembering' through the experiences at the village (at least emotionally - so by the end she would have 'awakened' to something more like how she used to be & be able to resume where they left off before the timeskip - and when Rei returns she'll sort of have a substitute experience for the childhood she never got to have with all the implicit learning that comes with that) or see her as an all new 'heartwarming orphan trope' who is basically an overgrown child, a reverse of Asuka & Mari maybe. But I don't see where you get "mother" from unless it's from conflating her with Yui. If anything, the Suzuharas seem to take a parent-like role for both. Rei doesn't really do any 'caretaking' nor would she have the capacity as she's basically just learning about everything. The sort of lines she gets are really like a 2 year old randomly comforting a crying person in that naive, sincere way that they have. It's mostly her innocent perspective & the example of her trying to live life as much as she could that get Shinji to act. It's an arc about actually rediscovering the present moment & the basic joys & just experiences of life that we often can't see because of the pain of the past or the burdens of the future.

[]: Re: miyamura interviews translation             Quote Post by Konja7 » Sat Apr 24, 2021 9:50 pm Kendrix wrote: I never said anything about "lover". Why is Friend not enough? Not to mention that she*s like the one person who's still around Shinji's age (if anything, ReiQ comes off younger) so she's the furthest from 'mother' out of anyone.

[]: Re: miyamura interviews translation             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Apr 24, 2021 9:56 pm That's awfully reductive tho, like it doesn't matter how she lived, what she decided & chose to do in that time, only that she got fridge to motivate a dude or teach him a lesson. Anyone can die; It's the life that you can learn from.

[]: Re: miyamura interviews translation             Quote Post by Konja7 » Sat Apr 24, 2021 10:03 pm Kendrix wrote: That's awfully reductive tho, like it doesn't matter how she lived, what she decided & chose to do in that time, only that she got fridge to motivate a dude or teach him a lesson. Anyone can die; It's the life that you can learn from.

[]: Re: miyamura interviews translation             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Sat Apr 24, 2021 10:18 pm Well, we found ourselves at a crossroads. The definition of "mother" is not concrete. It varies from person to person because we are all different. Most importantly, our mothers are not the same. I relate the word "mother" with kindness, care, patience, love and so on. I always felt some maternal vibrations coming from Ayanami. If I'm not mistaken, Shinji even talks about it in the manga (volume 5). Of course, I'm talking about Ayanami II, not Q. The latter indeed feels more like a little sister (someone who is discovering the world at her own pace, while also being with you). However, in this specific scene, the way she approached Shinji reminded me of a mother. Was that the intention? I don't know. I haven't even watched the movie. I can't form an opinion on it. Yet, this was how I felt while reading the transcript. As for Asuka, I don't see her actions as being "maternal", but some people can. I think she's more like a good friend than anything. Shikinami is doing her best to help and she indeed force-feeds him. She is also not afraid to throw reality in his face. In the end, I believe that she contributed to Shinji's recovery in her own "unique way". She's like that friend who cares about you, but won't stand for your bullshit. On the contrary, he/she will encourage you to face your problems and become a better person. Kendrix wrote: They're basically two random stray orphans whom the village people take in at the same time, she adapts slightly faster but she's still fundamentally another kid that's being taken care of & their getting to know that unfamiliar place at the same time (in contrasting ways)

[]: Re: miyamura interviews translation             Quote Post by Konja7 » Sat Apr 24, 2021 10:24 pm In twitter, I read an interpretation that Rei is the "mother" and Kaworu is the "father". That said, I agree that Rei Q doesn't give "mother" vibes. She has the innocence of a child.

[]: Re: miyamura interviews translation             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Apr 24, 2021 10:49 pm Konja7 wrote: In itself, I don't think Rei Q helps Shinji in his recovery, because they don't have many interactions.

[]: Re: miyamura interviews translation             Quote Post by Konja7 » Sat Apr 24, 2021 11:48 pm Kendrix wrote: Previously that would not have been a direction of interpretation that I would've leaned into, but if you've seen that partial clip of the last scene, it seems to be going for a 'recognition' sort of vibe with how the plugsuit gets discolored when the life support gives out & Shinji's expressions, like he's thinking, "Oh crap you were here all along" and then, plopp.

[]: Re: miyamura interviews translation             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Sat Apr 24, 2021 11:51 pm After thinking about what you said, I decided to revisit the transcript and see if my mind would have changed. Surprisingly, it did. It's really hard to tell what's happening in a movie just by its transcript. That is to say, all the nuances provided by the visual and sound spectra of the film are lost. Therefore, this isn't my final say on the matter. Anyway, I was too attached to the idea of Ayanami looking for Shinji and end up ignoring the dimension of her innocence. Yeah, she went after him and motivated him in a very affectionate way. That's something I would expect from a mother. That said, Ayanami approached the situation from a different place. You're right. She acts like a kid that's trying her best to help. Well, she did just that. Shinji did not "recover" simply because Ayanami went after him. Instead, he got better because she made him see the obvious. That is, his friends (Asuka, Toji, Kensuke and Hikari) still cared about him. Sure, he dropped the ball. That said, they still like him. Ayanami's innocent way of looking at the world was what helped Shinji to open his mind. I think it's cool how Ayanami asked Shinji if he also protected the village. The interactions between the three pilots seem to be very important. We have an adult, a teenager and a "child" into the mix.

Article: miyamura interviews translation
 trying her best to help. Well, she did just that. Shinji did not "recover" simply because Ayanami went after him. Instead, he got better because she made him see the obvious. That is, his friends (Asuka, Toji, Kensuke and Hikari) still cared about him. Sure, he dropped the ball. That said, they still like him. Ayanami's innocent way of looking at the world was what helped Shinji to open his mind. I think it's cool how Ayanami asked Shinji if he also protected the village.
